{
  Diagnostics: [
    {
      Id: DBVO006,
      Severity: Error,
      Message: The property 'Name' has [SequenceEquality] but does not implement IEnumerable/IEnumerator. Consider using [IncludeInEquality] instead.,
      Location: (9,19)
    },
    {
      Id: DBVO006,
      Severity: Error,
      Message: The property 'Count' has [SequenceEquality] but does not implement IEnumerable/IEnumerator. Consider using [IncludeInEquality] instead.,
      Location: (12,16)
    }
  ],
  GeneratedSources: [
    {
      HintName: Product.g.cs,
      Content:
// <auto-generated/>
#nullable enable
using System;
using System.Collections.Generic;
using System.Linq;

namespace TestNamespace
{
    partial class Product
    {
        /// <summary>
        /// Determines whether this Product is equal to another based on their values.
        /// </summary>
        protected override bool EqualsCore(Product other)
        {
            if (!SequenceEquals(Count, other.Count, true, true)) return false;
            if (!SequenceEquals(Name, other.Name, true, true)) return false;
            return true;
        }

        /// <summary>
        /// Returns a hash code for this value object's values.
        /// </summary>
        protected override int GetHashCodeCore()
        {
            var hashCode = new HashCode();

            AddSequenceHashCode(Count, ref hashCode, true, true);
            AddSequenceHashCode(Name, ref hashCode, true, true);

            return hashCode.ToHashCode();
        }

        private static bool SequenceEquals<T>(IEnumerable<T>? first, IEnumerable<T>? second, bool orderMatters, bool deepEquality)
        {
            if (ReferenceEquals(first, second)) return true;
            if (first is null || second is null) return false;

            var comparer = (!deepEquality && !typeof(T).IsValueType) ? (IEqualityComparer<T>)ReferenceEqualityComparerAdapter<T>.Instance : EqualityComparer<T>.Default;
            if (orderMatters)
                return first.SequenceEqual(second, comparer);

            var counts = new Dictionary<T, int>(comparer);
            foreach (var x in first) counts[x] = counts.TryGetValue(x, out var c) ? c + 1 : 1;
            foreach (var y in second)
            {
                if (!counts.TryGetValue(y, out var c)) return false;
                if (c == 1) counts.Remove(y); else counts[y] = c - 1;
            }
            return counts.Count == 0;
        }

        private static void AddSequenceHashCode<T>(IEnumerable<T>? source, ref HashCode hashCode, bool orderMatters, bool deepEquality)
        {
            if (source is null) return;
            if (orderMatters)
            {
                foreach (var item in source) hashCode.Add(item); return;
            }
            var comparer = (!deepEquality && !typeof(T).IsValueType) ? (IEqualityComparer<T>)ReferenceEqualityComparerAdapter<T>.Instance : EqualityComparer<T>.Default;
            var counts = new Dictionary<T, int>(comparer);
            foreach (var x in source) counts[x] = counts.TryGetValue(x, out var c) ? c + 1 : 1;
            foreach (var kvp in counts.OrderBy(kvp => comparer.GetHashCode(kvp.Key)))
            {
                hashCode.Add(kvp.Value);
                hashCode.Add(kvp.Key);
            }
        }

        private sealed class ReferenceEqualityComparerAdapter<T> : IEqualityComparer<T>
        {
            public static readonly ReferenceEqualityComparerAdapter<T> Instance = new();
            public bool Equals(T x, T y) => ReferenceEquals(x, y);
            public int GetHashCode(T obj) => System.Runtime.CompilerServices.RuntimeHelpers.GetHashCode(obj!);
        }

    }
}

    }
  ]
}
